user_outcome: Committed status history enforces maintainability phase order, exact dispatch budgets, bounded cycle transitions, Coach authority, and immutable lifecycle fields without adapter-specific inference.
rationale: This slice owns C-06 and makes role authority a deterministic state machine over committed C-05 evidence. It reads only public technical lifecycle records, logs identities rather than prompt/report bodies, performs no network/model call itself, and offers no UI surface. History validation is one first-parent pass with hash/set prefix checks, O(history records plus ledger entries), and must reject rather than repair ambiguous state.
in_scope: Enumerate every committed version of the physical status.json path along first-parent history, validate each exact schema and C-05 report identity, and require every later reports array to retain each earlier array as an exact prefix.

Keep start_commit immutable after first non-null value; keep cycle monotonic and bounded to 0 or 1; keep adjudication immutable once present; and preserve implementation_head according to the newest valid PASS/FAIL transition without reconstructing missing history.

Enforce cycle-0 Implementer preflight exactly once; a PASS ends Implementer review, while a FAIL permits one bounded remediation followed by exactly one closure review. No closure is legal without the earlier preflight FAIL.

Permit exactly one fresh Verifier authoritative review only after the current-cycle Implementer PASS, over the same pinned implementation head; Verifier PASS preserves passed authority and Verifier FAIL follows C-06's cycle-specific transition without self-repair or rerun.

Permit the human Coach to adjudicate a completed cycle-0 authoritative failure exactly once as resume_in_scope or re_slice; resume requires two unique cited report invocation IDs and corresponding fingerprints, non-empty permitted touchpoints that are a subset of the ratified spec touchpoints, and advances to cycle 1.
Enforce cycle-1 as the final Implementer/fresh-Verifier budget; any cycle-1 blocking failure or any re_slice decision reaches re_slice_required with no second resume or waiver path.

Expose pure ValidateHistory, AllowedNext, DispatchBudget, and ApplyCoachDecision operations consumed later by one coordinator; return structured reasons and proposed next authority but perform no model dispatch, record write, commit, push, or adapter routing.
out_of_scope: Constructing semantic scope belongs to S07 and validating full report blob identity belongs to S08; this FSM consumes already validated C-04/C-05 inputs.
Public review/adjudication commands and atomic write/commit/push recovery belong to S13-maintainability-engine-cutover.
Role-loop routing and session restart orchestration belongs to S14-role-lifecycle-recovery.
Creating rollback/replacement slices or applying re-plan mutations belongs to S12 and S17; this slice only returns the required terminal lifecycle disposition.

effort_complexity.rationale: The FSM composes immutable committed history, exact report prefixes, role/phase budgets, two bounded cycles, and Coach-only adjudication across eleven files.

risks.description: Validation checks only the latest status and accepts a reset, removed report, changed start, or rewritten adjudication hidden in history.
risks.mitigation: Read every first-parent status version and compare immutable fields plus exact report prefixes before evaluating the newest state.

risks.description: A role adapter can obtain another dispatch by relabeling phase, cycle, or state.
risks.mitigation: Derive AllowedNext and DispatchBudget solely from validated history/C-05 tuples; caller-supplied phase is a requested transition that must equal the single legal next edge.

risks.description: Coach resume expands the ratified boundary or cycle 1 receives another waiver.
risks.mitigation: Validate permitted touchpoints as a non-empty subset of spec.touchpoints, freeze adjudication, and make every second resume or post-cycle-1 failure transition deterministically to re_slice_required.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN TestLifecycleHistoryFSMRealGit evaluates real first-parent histories covering preflight PASS, preflight FAIL plus closure, authoritative PASS/FAIL, Coach decisions, and cycle 1 THE SYSTEM SHALL return exactly C-06's legal state, next role/phase, remaining dispatch budget, and terminal disposition.

IF committed history changes or clears start_commit, removes/reorders/rewrites a report prefix, lowers cycle, mutates adjudication, skips/repeats/reorders a phase, appends after a terminal entry, or disagrees with C-05 identity THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL fail history validation without proposing an authoritative transition.

WHEN cycle 0 has an authoritative FAIL and the Coach submits resume_in_scope citing the two required unique invocation IDs and fingerprints with permitted touchpoints strictly inside spec.touchpoints THE SYSTEM SHALL produce one immutable resume_approved cycle-1 adjudication; IF any citation or boundary differs, or a second resume is requested, THE SYSTEM SHALL reject it.

WHILE a lifecycle is in cycle 0 or cycle 1 THE SYSTEM SHALL allow at most one Implementer preflight, one closure only after preflight FAIL, and one fresh Verifier authoritative dispatch only after Implementer PASS; Coach decisions consume no model dispatch and cycle 1 exposes no further resume budget.

IF the Coach chooses re_slice in cycle 0 or any blocking maintainability result exhausts cycle 1 THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return re_slice_required, clear active implementation authority as C-06 requires, preserve the full append-only history, and expose no waiver, reset, or synthetic PASS transition.
